title: Centaurea phaeopappa
intro: Centaurea phaeopappa, also known as the Phaeopappus knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the aster family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea phaeopappa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Centaurea phaeopappa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Centaurea phaeopappa belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Centaurea. As a part of the genus, Centaurea phaeopappa is classified under the order Asterales and the clade Rosids. Within the family Asteraceae, it is placed in the subfamily Carduoideae. The species is native to the Mediterranean region.
